summary: U.S. equities closed sharply lower on Friday as stocks retreated from record highs set earlier in the week. The S&P 500 fell 1.24% to 7,408.50, the Nasdaq dropped 1.54% to 26,225.14, and the Dow lost 537 points to 49,526.17. The selloff was triggered by rising bond yields—the 30-year Treasury hit 5.114% and the 10-year reached 4.54%, both 12-month highs—as markets priced a 44% chance of a Fed rate hike by December. President Trump's two-day summit with Xi Jinping in Beijing ended without major breakthroughs on Iran peace efforts, keeping oil above $109 per barrel and stoking persistent inflation fears. Meanwhile, crypto markets gave back gains despite the Senate Banking Committee advancing the CLARITY Act 15-9 on Thursday. Bitcoin slipped below $80,000 to $78,000 levels, Ethereum fell to $2,250, and over $500 million in leveraged long positions were liquidated as bond yields spiked and risk appetite cooled.

signals.title: Bond yields surge to 12-month highs, Fed hike odds jump to 44%
signals.desc: The 30-year Treasury yield hit 5.114% and the 10-year reached 4.54%, pulling capital from equities and crypto as markets reprice Fed policy toward tightening by December.

signals.title: Trump-Xi summit ends without Iran peace progress, oil tops $109
signals.desc: Two-day Beijing talks concluded with business deals but no breakthrough on Iran war, with Brent crude rising over 2% to $109 and fueling persistent inflation concerns.

signals.title: Crypto liquidation cascade wipes $500M in leveraged longs
signals.desc: Bitcoin fell below $80,000 and Ethereum slipped to $2,250 despite CLARITY Act passage, as over $500 million in long positions were flushed amid bond selloff and risk-off sentiment.

signals.title: Senate advances CLARITY Act 15-9, but market rally fades in hours
signals.desc: Bipartisan passage of the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act initially sparked a crypto rally, but gains unwound within four hours as macro headwinds overwhelmed regulatory optimism.

signals.title: Cisco surges 15% on AI networking boom, cutting 4,000 jobs
signals.desc: Cisco stock popped on surging AI data center orders and a networking 'supercycle,' offsetting tech sector weakness with strong demand visibility.

verdict: Markets face a trifecta of headwinds—spiking yields, stalled Iran diplomacy, and fading crypto momentum—with bond vigilantes driving a sharp repricing of Fed dovishness.
